## PAGE 01939: (2026x Refresh1) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard

- page_id: `303998549`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998549/2026x+Refresh1+Typical+Resources+Deployment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:43.994+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** is to deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.

To open the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with CV-5 typical table' space=''] (DoDAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with StV-5 typical table' space=''] (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with NCV-5 typical table' space=''] (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space=''] (UAF) table.
2. Double-click a cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Select Resources

In this step, select the Resourcesrequired by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing Resources or create new ones.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### In Service date specification

In this step, you can specify the In Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be released and used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default, the Today date is selected.

If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase) and the date selection steps are not available.

The In Service date for the Resource usage can not be later than the Out Of Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard only if the start date is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Out Of Service date specification

In this step, you can specify the Out Of Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be withdrawn. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.

The Out Of Service date for the Resourceusage can not be earlier than the In Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard if the end date is not specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestones with the same chosen dates already exist, they are reused.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with CV-5 typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with StV-5 typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='']

## PAGE 01940: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Changes of element type and metaclass

- page_id: `303996797`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996797/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+of+element+type+and+metaclass
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:23.410+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.

| Element | UAF 1.0 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.2 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AssetRole | Element | Property |
| Desirer | Class | Element |
| WholeLifeEnterprise | Class | InstanceSpecification |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.0 [Type] | UAF 1.2 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity::activityPerformableUnderCondition | Condition | ActualCondition |
| EnterpriseGoal::benefit | String | ValueItem |
| Measurement::environmentalContext | Condition | ActualCondition |
| LocationHolder::requiredEnvironment | Environment | ActualEnvironment |
| CapabilityConfiguration::doctrine | Constraint | StandardOperationalActivity |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996808 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01941: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `303996808`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996808/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:24.895+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

##### Changed Property Types

44

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for :

- Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.
- Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created. I f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions, the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

##### The Security Property renamed

In UAF 1.3, the Security Property is renamed to Operational Information Role and the new Resource Information Role is implemented. The Operational Information Role is used by the Operational Assets and the Resource Information Role is used by the Resource Assets.

- Operational Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information.
- Resource Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information.

If Operational Agent and Operational Information (in UAF 1.2 grouped by Operational Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Operational Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was any of the Operational Asset specializations.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If Resource Performer and Resource Information (in UAF 1.2 grouped by Resource Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Resource Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Operational Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Known Resource exception

Known Resource exception

The Known Resource is both: Resource Asset and Operational Asset. After the migration, the Security Property (owned by the Know Resource) is changed depending on its type:

- If the type was Resource Information, the Security Property becomes Resource Information Role.
- If the type was Operational Information, the Security Property becomes Operational Information Role.

##### Enduring Task removed

Enduring Task element was removed, so after the migration, it become Strategic Phase.

##### Capability For Task removed

Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse).

##### Changes for properties of ActualEnterprisePhase

- Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Operational Architecture element.
- Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Resource Architecture element.
- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal
- **Concern**property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between *Actual Enterprise Phase* and Concern

##### Changes for *Concern property*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Concern

##### Changes for property of *Enterprise Goal*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal

##### Changes for *property of* *Enterprise Vision*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision

##### Changes for properties of Desired Effect and Achieved Effect

- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect is removed.
- DesiredEffect:: achievedEffect is removed.
- After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between **desires** target and **achieves** target if AchievedEffect:: **desiredEffect** and DesiredEffect::**achievedEffect** were linked with each other.

##### Consumes renamed

In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports and the direction is reversed.

##### Specializations of Capable Element updated

In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, Exhibits relationship was removed and Phases will be created.

##### Changed Property Types

In earlier versions, the Benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2 benefit property has value Item for its value. After migration, the Value Item element will be created based on benefit value.

##### Changed for Whole Life Enterprise

In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Resource</em><em> Architecture </em>element.</li><li data-uuid="7a1efb7c-bb7e-4ed9-b6b9-8f3ac3d8c035"><strong>Vision</strong> property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Vision</em></li><li data-uuid="c425de48-07e5-42ff-a4ba-f2609c5d164d"><strong>Goal</strong> property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Goal</em></li><li data-uuid="71cacf6e-a3b8-4dfb-8bf8-8a09b8876569"><span><strong>Concern </strong>property was removed, so after the migration </span><em>Phases</em><span> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and </span><em>Concern</em></li></ul><h3>Changes for <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Concern property</span></em></h3><ul><li data-uuid="75972ff8-8a57-45e0-adba-5b97a5b54db1"><strong>Enterprise Phase </strong>property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Concern</em></li></ul><h3>Changes for property of <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Enterprise Goal</span></em></h3><ul><li data-uuid="4ed405d5-598b-421c-84b5-03fd375fd598"><strong>Enterprise Phase </strong>property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Goal</em></li></ul><h3>Changes for <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">property of </span></em> <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Enterprise Vision </span></em></h3><ul><li data-uuid="4ac18bf8-51cc-40f4-8d3e-7544e59bc861"><strong>Enterprise Phase </strong>property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Vision</em></li></ul><h3>Changes for properties of  Desired Effect and Achieved Effect </h3><ul><li data-uuid="90dffe73-510d-41c7-bbf4-05c451e9067b">AchievedEffect:: <strong>desiredEffect </strong>is removed.</li><li data-uuid="32d3e340-cb01-4d10-b95d-26eda5b062a1">DesiredEffect::<strong>achievedEffect</strong> is removed.</li><li data-uuid="79e4de2f-2c70-453c-bfc9-eb82771e4162"><p>After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between <strong>desires</strong> target and <strong>achieves</strong> target if AchievedEffect:: <strong>desiredEffect</strong> and DesiredEffect::<strong>achievedEffect</strong> were linked with each other. </p></li></ul><h3>Consumes renamed</h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports and the direction is reversed. </p><h3>Specializations of Capable Element updated</h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, Exhibits relationship was removed and Phases will be created. </p><h3>Changed Property Types</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In earlier versions, the Benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2  benefit property has value Item for its value. After migration, the Value Item element will be created based on benefit value. </span></p><h3>Changed for Whole Life Enterprise</h3><p>In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase. </p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996801 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01942: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `303996801`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996801/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:23.956+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Operational Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information allowing them to own Operational Information Role. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Resource Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information allowing them to own Resource Information Roles |
| Resource Information Role* | A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements. |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange. The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an &quot;end effect&quot;.</td></tr><tr><td>Capability Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996799 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01943: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `303996799`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996799/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:23.679+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.0 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Capability Property | Capability Role |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Security Property* | Operational Information Role |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element | Property UAF 1.0 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| OperationalExchange | trustlevel | trustLevel |
| ResponsibleFor | ResponsibleRoleKind | responsibleRoleKind |
| Architecture | desribedBy | describedBy |
| ArchitecturalDescription | viewPoint | viewpoint |

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- Condition::conditionKind
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:ref="1873eb91-2884-4c92-98c8-2919269e7610"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1873eb91-2884-4c92-98c8-2919269e7610">Condition::conditionKind</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p>ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal</li><li>ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern</li><li>AchievedEffect:: <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997261 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01944: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Changes in diagrams, tables, and matrices

- page_id: `303997261`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997261/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+in+diagrams+tables+and+matrices
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:58.398+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

New diagrams, tables, and matrices:

- Architecture Management diagram
- Security Controls diagram
- Security Constraints table
- Services Role-based Connectivity Table
- Services Connectivity Table
- Services Connectivity Matrix
- Strategic Constraint table
- Strategic Information diagram
- Strategic Motivation diagram
- Strategic Processes
- Functions to Operational Activities Matrix
- Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Project Mapping Matrix

The following table shows how the tables, diagrams, and matrices were renamed consistent with the UAF 1.2 support.

| Name in UAF 1.1 | Name in UAF 1.2 |
| --- | --- |
| Capabilities to Actual Enduring Tasks Mapping Matrix | Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping Matrix |
| Service Specifications to Capabilities Mapping matrix | Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix |
| Operational Activities to Service Specifications Mapping matrix | Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix |
| Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix | Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix |
| Operational Interaction Scenarios | Operational Sequences |
| Services Interaction Scenarios | Services Sequences |
| Personnel Interaction Scenarios | Personnel Sequences |
| Resources Interaction Scenarios | Resources Sequences |
| Operational Interaction Scenarios BPD | Operational Sequences BPD |
| Personnel Interaction Scenarios BPD | Personnel Sequences BPD |
| Resources Interaction Scenarios BPD | Resources Sequences BPD |
| Conceptual Data Model | Strategic Information |
| Logical Data Model | Operational Information |
| Physical Data Model | Resources Information |
| Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart | Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart |

Updated diagrams, tables, and matrices:

| Name | Note |
| --- | --- |
| SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix | In UAF 1.2, Associations were changed to Service Exchanges |
| Implementation matrix | The Implements relationship is available between the following client and supplier pairs:Resource Performer and Operational AgentFunction and Operational Activity or Service FunctionResource Interface and Service Interface or Operational InterfaceResource Connector and Operational Connector or Resource ConnectorResource Exchange and Operational ExchangeResource Role and Operational RoleResource Information and Operational informationArchitecture or Operational Activity and Actual Strategic PhaseResource Service and ServiceOperational Information and Strategic Information |
| SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills ForecastSvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills ForecastStdV-2 Standards ForecastNSV-9 Technology ForecastNTV-2 Technical Standards ForecastSV-9 Technology & Skills ForecastTV-2 Standards ForecastP1 Resource Types Technology ForecastA8 Standards ForecastStandards RoadmapResources ForecastPersonnel Forecast | It is possible to add the Actual Strategic Phase as a column in the table.In the 3rd step of the wizard, you can select Actual Strategic Phases. |
| Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping MatrixLr Responsibility MatrixAcV-1 Responsibility MatrixPV-1 Responsibility MatrixNPV-1 Responsibility Matrix | The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources. |
| Actual Resources Taxonomy Matrix | The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources. |
| Resources to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| Operational Activity to Services Traceability | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| UPDM Capability Behavioral Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Capability Structural Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Implementation Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Activity Decomposition Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Activity Implementation Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Organization Structure Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Resource Performer Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |

## PAGE 01945: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Changes of element metaclass and type

- page_id: `303997295`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997295/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+of+element+metaclass+and+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:00.647+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element | UAF 1.1 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.2 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| WholeLifeEnterprise | Class | InstanceSpecification |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.1 [Type] | UAF 1.2 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| EnterpriseGoal::benefit | String | ValueItem |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997263 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01946: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `303997263`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997263/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:58.585+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Enduring Task removed

The Enduring Task element was removed, so it became the Strategic Phase after the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability For Task removed

Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration, Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of ActualEnterprisePhase

- Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Operational Architecture element.
- Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Resource Architecture element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal
- **Concern**property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between *Actual Enterprise Phase* and Concern

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of Desired Effect and Achieved Effect

- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect is removed.
- DesiredEffect:: achievedEffect is removed.
- After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between **desires** target and **achieves** target if AchievedEffect:: **desiredEffect** and DesiredEffect::**achievedEffect** were linked with each other.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Consumes renamed

In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports, and the direction is reversed.

##### Risk Owner removed

The Risk Owner property is removed, so after the migration, it will be added to the ToDo property in the Specification window of the Risk element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specializations of Capable Element updated

In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes Property Types

In earlier versions, the benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2, the benefit property has a value Item for its value. After migration, a value item element will be created based on benefit value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for Whole Life Enterprise

In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Enduring Task removed</h3><p>The Enduring Task element was removed, so it became the Strategic Phase after the migration. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="enduringTask_to_strategicPhase.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Capability For Task removed</h3><p>Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration, Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse). </p><p><ac:image ac:height="182"><ri:attachment ri:filename="capability_forTask_to_phases.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Changes for properties of  ActualEnterprisePhase</h3><ul><li data-uuid="150ae2f1-2af7-4d13-b2f4-df0e42aa1816"><strong>Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase</strong> property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Operational </em><em>Architecture</em> element.</li><li data-uuid="0550a0b0-6e7a-4fa2-b50f-a5dd5c46f220"><strong>Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase</strong> property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Resource</em><em> Architecture </em>element.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="AEP_Implements_OpArch_ResArch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="0037a011-6c26-424e-86f5-76ad58237f90"><strong>Vision</strong> property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Vision</em></li><li data-uuid="eabecb82-b82a-4eeb-bd58-f3c732fe18e8"><strong>Goal</strong> property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Goal</em></li><li data-uuid="b6c0983f-b218-4b38-b4ac-bb2312237911"><span><strong>Concern </strong>property was removed, so after the migration </span><em>Phases</em><span> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and </span><em>Concern<br /></em></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="AEP_Phases_OpArch_ResArch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Changes for properties of  Desired Effect and Achieved Effect </h3><ul><li data-uuid="00fd077d-24cc-4606-a720-f049db7f8b19">AchievedEffect:: <strong>desiredEffect</strong> is removed.</li><li data-uuid="0dac3971-ec96-4d1c-a273-6ef47c0a9fd7">DesiredEffect::<strong>achievedEffect</strong> is removed.</li><li data-uuid="74a92532-e3f6-4565-a383-17365b272d85"><p>After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between <strong>desires</strong> target and <strong>achieves</strong> target if AchievedEffect:: <strong>desiredEffect</strong> and DesiredEffect::<strong>achievedEffect</strong> were linked with each other. </p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="achieves_desires.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Consumes renamed</h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports, and the direction is reversed. </p><h3>Risk Owner removed</h3><p>The Risk Owner property is removed, so after the migration, it will be added to the ToDo property in the Specification window of the Risk element.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="243"><ri:attachment ri:filename="riskOwner_removed.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Specializations of Capable Element updated</h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exhibits_to_phases.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Changes Property Types</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In earlier versions, the benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2, the benefit property has a value Item for its value. After migration, a value item element will be created based on benefit value. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="234"><ri:attachment ri:filename="benefit_to_valueItem.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3>Changes for Whole Life Enterprise</h3><p>In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase. </p><p><ac:image ac:height="86"><ri:attachment ri:filename="whole_life_enterprise_to_strategicPhase.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. ## PAGE 01947: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. New elements
````

- page_id: `303997259`

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997257 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01948: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `303997257`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997257/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:58.057+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.1 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Property UAF 1.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |

****

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

colspan="1">Achieves</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Desired Effect</td><td colspan="1">Desires</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Enterprise Phase</td><td colspan="1">Strategic Phase</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Organization In Enterprise</td><td colspan="1">Organization In Phase</td></tr></tbody></table><p>The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th><p>Property UAF 1.1 [name]</p></th><th><p>Property UAF 1.2 [name]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1">Information Role</td><td colspan="1">Operational Information Role</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Data Role</td><td colspan="1">Resource Information Role</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Specification Role</td><td colspan="1">Service Role</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong> </strong></p><p><strong>Removed properties:</strong></p><ul><li>Concern::enterprisePhase</li><li><p>ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase</p></li><li>ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase</li><li>ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision</li><li>ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal</li><li>ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern</li><li>AchievedEffect:: ## PAGE 01949: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. Changes in diagrams, tables, and matrices

- page_id: `303992269`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992269/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.2+to+UAF+1.3.+Changes+in+diagrams+tables+and+matrices
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:48.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**New diagrams:**

| Name | Location |
| --- | --- |
|  | You can create it under the Strategic Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Operational Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Operational Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Resources Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g, Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters). |

**Renamed:**

The following table shows how the tables, diagrams, and matrices were renamed to be consistent with UAF 1.3 support.

| UAF 1.2 | UAF 1.3 |
| --- | --- |
| Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table | Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992271 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01951: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `303992271`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992271/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.2+to+UAF+1.3.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:48.868+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### UAF 1.2 Enterprise Mission to UAF 1.3 Actual Mission

###### In a single case

The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Mission.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### In multiple cases

- The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Mission [ATTACHMENT filename='enterprise_mission_to_actual_mission_multiple1.png']
- The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Strategic Phase [ATTACHMENT filename='enterprise_mission_to_actual_mission_multiple2.png']

##### Location Holder

###### Asset

- The Asset was removed from the Location Holder subtypes.
- The requiredElement and physicalLocation information was added to the ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resource Role and Operational Role

- The requiredElement property type is changed from Actual Environment to Condition.
- The information from the requiredElement property was moved to the ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Phaseable element and Phases relationship

Changes of Phases relationship supplier Phaseable element:

- Opportunity was removed fromthe Phaseable Element subtypes.
- Motivational Element added to Phaseable Element subtypes (Opportunity is a subtype of Motivational Element.

Changes of Phases relationship client Actual Strategic Phase

- The Actual Strategic Phase derived property phasesOpportunity was renamed to phasesMotivationalElement .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Activity

- The ActivityPerformableUnderCondition property was removed
  - The property information was added to the ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Activity changes
  - Activity was removed from the Measureable element subtypes.
    - Properties MeasurementSet and ActualMeasurementSet are removed.
  - Activity was added as a subtype of a Property Set.
    - An Activity can own measurements directly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example 1. Measurement Set

Measurement is created for an Activity and typed by the Measurement Set (removed property value)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example 2. Measurement Set and Actual Measurement Set

- Measurement is created for an Activity and typed by the removed property Measurement Set.
- Actual Measurement is created for an Activity, and a new Actual Property Set is created with filled-in slots for the removed Actual Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example 3. Actual Measurement Set

The information is added to the ToDo property if the Actual Measurement Set (removed property value) was without a type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Standard and Actual Organization

ratifiedBy property:

- ratifiedBy property type was changed from Actual Organization to Actual Organizational Resource
- The property value after the migration is not affected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

-----------------------------

##### Modeling suggestion

Here is a suggestion on how to model with Opposable element and Designation Kind.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

## PAGE 01952: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. New elements

- page_id: `303992267`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992267/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.2+to+UAF+1.3.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:47.919+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.3 | MetaClass | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Actual Mission | Instance Specification | A particular mission to accomplish the assigned mission objectives. |
| Actual Mission Phase | Instance Specification | A particular phase in the accomplishment of an overall actual mission. |
| Actual Mission Scenario | Instance Specification | A particular Mission Scenario within which an Actual Mission is to be accomplished. |
| Actual Mission Vignette | Instance Specification | A particular Mission Vignette within which an Actual Mission is to be accomplished. |
| Conflicts With | Comment | An annotation that indicates UAF elements acting against or in opposition to each other. This means that there are elements in the model that disagree about something important or are incompatible with each other in some way. |
| Defines | Abstraction | A tuple that links the StrategicPhase and the Activity that implements it. |
| Designation Kind | Enimeration | An enumerated type that defines the kind of structural element with respect to the purpose, intent, and capabilities of that element. |
| Doctrine | Class | A formal expression of military knowledge and thought, accepted as being relevant at a given time, which covers the nature of conflict, the preparation of the military forces for conflict, and the method of engaging in conflict to achieve success. it is descriptive rather than prescriptive, requiring judgment in application. |
| Mission | Class | The task, together with the purpose, that clearly indicates the action to be taken and the reasoning behind the mission. |
| Mission Engineering Thread | Activity | A sequence of end-to-end mission-related functions, actions, and events presented as a series of steps that implement a mission thread. |
| Mission Task | Activity | A clearly defined activity specifically to be assigned to a system, person, or organization that must be completed. |
| Mission Task Action | Call Behavior Action | The usage of a MissionTask within a MissionThread. |
| Mission Thread | Activity | A sequence of end-to-end mission tasks, activities, and events presented as a series of steps to achieve a mission. |
| Mission Scenario | Data Type | Description of the geographical location and time frame of the overall conflict. A scenario includes information such as threat and friendly politico-military contexts and backgrounds, assumptions, constraints, limitations, strategic objectives, and other planning considerations. |
| Mission Vignette | Data Type | Description of the geographical location and time frame of the overall conflict. A vignette includes information such as threat and friendly politico-military contexts and backgrounds, assumptions, constraints, limitations, strategic objectives, and other planning considerations. It can represent small, ideally self-contained parts of a scenario. |
| Opposes | Dependency | A tuple that indicates an open clash between two opposing groups (or individuals). This means that elements in the model are fighting against each other or are providing strong resistance to progress towards some goals. Note to entry: By individual, this does not indicate instances in the model. |
| Opposable Element | Element | A type of element that is categorized to allow for it to have an Opposes relationship to some other opposableelement |
| RuleKind: Assumption | Enumeration Literal | Indicates that the element is accepted as true or as certain to happen, without proof. |
| Mission Kind | Enumeration | An enumerated list of kinds of missions. Its enumeration literals are:National Policy - A broad course of action or statements of guidance adopted by the government at the national level in pursuit of national objectives.Theater Strategy - An overarching construct outlining a combatant commander’s vision for integrating and synchronizing military activities and operations with the other instruments of national power in order to achieve national strategic objectives. See also national military strategy; national securitystrategy; strategy.Campaign - A series of related major operations aimed at achieving strategic and operational objectives within a given time and space.Major Operation - A series of tactical actions (battles, engagements, strikes) conducted by combat forces, coordinated in time and place, to achieve strategic or operational objectives in an operational area.Battle - A hostile encounter between opposing military forces.Engagement - A tactical conflict, usually between opposing lower echelons maneuver forces. See also battle; campaign.Small Unit And Crew Action - Small unit and Crew Action is the combat deployment of platoons and smaller units in a particular strategic and logistic environment.Other - A different kind of mission. |
| ActualOrganizationalResource::ratifiedStandardsActualOrganizationalResource::publishedStandardsActualOrganizationalResource::createdStandards | Associations | ratifiedStandards : Standard [*] Standards that were ratified by this ActualOrganization.publishedStandards : Standard [*] A relationship between an actual organization and the standards published by that organizationcreatedStandards : Standard [*] A relationship between an actual organization and the standards created by that organization |
| Standard::publishedByStandard:: createdBy | Associations | publishedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource [*] An entity responsible for making the resource availablecreatedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource [*] The organization or creator of the standard or doctrine |

## PAGE 01953: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. Removed elements

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990942 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01954: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3

- page_id: `303990942`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990942/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.3
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:13.286+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:

- model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;
- model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;
- support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and
- improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML v1-based and tools that are based on other standards.

According to modeling needs, there are the following UAF templates for different purposes:

- UAF Project is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.
- EA with BPMN Project* - u sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.
- **UAF Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.
- **EA Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.
- **Blank UAF Project** - creates a blank UAF project.

Please make sure, that the UAF 1.3 Plugin is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='IL'].

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.3 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification.

The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:

| Viewpoint | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Architecture Management | Am | Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose. |
| Strategic | St | Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution. |
| Operational | Op | Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner. |
| Services | Sv | The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service withinMODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas. |
| Personnel | Ps | Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time. |
| Resources | Rs | Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML v1 or UML. |
| Security | Sc | Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves). |
| Projects | Pj | Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects. |
| Standards | Sd | MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements. |
| Actual Resources | Ar | The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations. |

The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:

| Aspect | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Motivation | Mv | Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls. |
| Taxonomy | Tx | Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. |
| Structure | Sr | Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts. |
| Connectivity | Cn | Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements. |
| Processes | Pr | Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them. |
| States | St | Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions. |
| Sequences | Sq | Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario. |
| Information | If | Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues. |
| Parameters | Pm | Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems. |
| Constraints | Ct | Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure. |
| Roadmap | Rm | Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time. |
| Traceability | Tr | Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors. |

**UAF 1.3 viewpoints**

## PAGE 01957: (2026x Refresh1) UAF Behavior to structure synchronization

- page_id: `304000296`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000296/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+Behavior+to+structure+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:58.836+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity diagram with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies, and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solver actions to ensure the coherence of the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity diagrams with Activities.

600400

The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Operational / Resource / Service Role displayed in the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity Diagram to check for compatible

- Operational / Resource / Service Ports
- Operational / Resource / Service Connectors
- Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces
- Operational / Resource / Service Exchanges

To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., ports, interfaces) and behavioral elements (e.g., flows) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Connectivity Diagram and displayed in the**Validation Results** panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing behavior to the structure.

##### Checking behavior to structure synchronization

To initiate the behavior to structure synchronization check, ensure the Internal Connectivity Diagrams have

displayed,

).

To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model

1. In the toolbar of the <viewpoint name> * Internal Connectivity Diagram, click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools.
2. In the open drop-down menu, select Behavior to Structure Synchronization. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.

*** <Viewpoint name>** - Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).

**Continue reading**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000270 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01958: (2026x Refresh1) UAF element numbering

- page_id: `304000270`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000270/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+element+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:56.803+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide a generic numbering mechanism that can be applied to any elements created in the modeling tool. The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:

- Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.
- Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.
- Show element's place in a hierarchy.

For more information about the generic numbering mechanism and how to use it, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generic numbering mechanism' space=''] documentation.

The default numbering schemas can have two different numbering styles:

- conse **cutive** - this style is to number elements in one numbering level. Specifyonly one number part.
- multi-level - this style is to number elements in several numbering levels. Specify at least one number part for each numbering level. Top level elements will be numbered using the first number part. Elements in subsequent levels (directly or indirectly contained in the top level element) will be numbered using successive number parts.

To learn more about the numbering styles, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Numbering Scheme property values' space='UPDG'] documentation.

UAF numbering uses **multi-level** numbering style schema property.

#### UAF hierarchical numbering

Numbering of UAF motivational elements and information elements is hierarchical.

A hierarchical structure can only contain elements of the same type. Different element types cannot be mixed within the same hierarchy.

Motivational Elements:

- Driver (prefix is D)
- Opportunity (prefix is O)
- Challenge (prefix is C)
- Effect (prefix is E)

Information Elements:

- Operational Information (prefix is OI)
- Resource Information (prefix is RI)
- Strategic Information (prefix is SI)

#### UAF hierarchical numbering with dynamic prefixes

Numbering of UAF elements based on SysML v1 requirements is hierarchical or dynamic (when prefix differs, numbering adapts. numbers will change, but prefixes will be kept according to elements).

A hierarchical structure can contain elements of different type. For example, Enterprise Goal and Enterprise Objective can both be in the same hierarchical structure. The same is with the Security elements.

Strategic Elements:

- Enterprise Goal (prefix is EG)
- Enterprise Objective (prefix is EO)

Security Elements:

- Security Control (prefix is SC)
- Enhanced Security Control (prefix is ESC)
- Security Control Family (prefix is SCF)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generic numbering mechanism' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
