ac:name="">MultipleReports</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Generating multiple reports </h4><p>You can open multiple projects and generate multiple reports before the modeling tool shuts down. The <strong>generate</strong> command with the -properties option accepts multiple properties files as shown below.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>generate -properties &lt;file1.properties&gt; &lt;file2.properties&gt; &lt;file3.properties&gt;...</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>generate -properties “property1.properties” “property2.properties”</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f51f4e8-3acb-491d-b2d8-9f3171611a39"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Template</strong>, <strong>output</strong>, <strong>package</strong>, and <strong>project</strong> are the required data for each property file.</p><p>The following parameters are reset when finishing generating a report for each property file. Other parameter values affect subsequent property files, if there are no overridden values.</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">-project</p></li><li>-output</li><li>-template</li><li>-templatePath</li><li>-tag</li><li>-pVersion</li><li>-recursive</li><li>-category</li><li>-includeAuxiliary</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="417ea8b0-2a85-4a3b-87a0-f818565da74c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When large-size projects need to be loaded and closed for many property files, it takes longer time to generate reports. In this case, you can use<strong> -leaveprojectopen</strong> as an option to reduce memory for loading each project when starting to generate a new report.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a6aa847-76e1-46dd-bcc5-cd6809a96045"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -leaveprojectopen true -properties “property1.properties” “property2.properties”]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1da55347-e339-4281-b8fb-fb0ac99bba30"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><em>-leaveprojectopen</em> needs to be specified before<em> -properties.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Properties+filename">Properties filename</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers">Uploading generated reports to servers</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Syntax+rules">Syntax rules</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995067 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02047: (2026x Refresh1) Using the grid

- page_id: `303995067`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995067/2026x+Refresh1+Using+the+grid
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:04.593+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903695269

903695272

903695271

The grid helps to arrange diagram symbols on the diagram pane. By default, the grid is not visible.

To show the grid on the diagram, do one of the following

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Show Grid .
- From the diagram shortcut menu, select Show Grid .
- In the Diagram Properties dialog, set the Show Grid property to true .

To snap the paths to gridlines

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Snap Paths to Grid .

To snap the shapes to gridlines

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Snap Shapes to Grid .

To change the grid size

1. From the main menu, select View > Grid > Grid Size . The Grid Size dialog opens.
2. Enter a grid size between 2 and 30 (default is 10).
3. Click OK .

To change the grid style

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the Diagram group, from the Grid Style option drop-down list, select one of the following styles:
  - Dense
  - Sparse (default)

903695268

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993725 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02048: (2026x Refresh1) Using the Model Browser

- page_id: `303993725`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993725/2026x+Refresh1+Using+the+Model+Browser
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:53.317+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface > (2026x Refresh1) Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425704795

1425704797

1425704796

INLINE

Model Browser is displayed when at least one project is open. By default, the Model Browser is placed on the left side of the main window. It is possible to move the Model Browser to any place on the window. Also, all tabs can be viewed separately and you can set up the Model Browser according to your needs by hiding the desired tabs. 
 
To change the size of either part of the Model Browser

- Drag the bar that separates the two parts.

To zoom the Model Browser

1. Select a tab in the Model Browser.
2. Use one of the following shortcuts:

- 
  - For Mac

1. 
  1. 
    1. Cmd+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Cmd+Numpad +/-

- 
  - For Windows

1. 
  1. 
    1. Ctrl+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Ctrl+Numpad +/-

To change the Model Browser position

- Using the **Options** menu

1. 
  1. From the **Options** menu, select **Environment**. The **Environment Options** dialog opens.
  2. Click on the **Browser** option group.
  3. In the Browser environment options pane, change the **Browser Position** property to **Right** or **Left**.

- Using Toggle 
In the upper-right corner of the selected tab in the Model Browser, click the Toggle auto-hide [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or Toggle floating [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. This action changes the layout of the interface.

To close or reopen the desired tab of the Model Browser

- Using the **Window** menu, choose the Model Browser tab you want to close or open.

To reset all Model Browser tabs to the default position

- From the **Window** menu, choose the **Reset Windows Configuration** command.

To sort items in the Model Browser alphabetically

1. From the Options menu, select Environment. The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Click the Browser option group.
3. In the Browser options pane, set the Sort Alphabetically value to true (the default value is true).

1425704794

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991162 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02049: (2026x Refresh1) Using To Do property

- page_id: `303991162`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991162/2026x+Refresh1+Using+To+Do+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:25.043+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked "added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.

To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property

1. From the main toolbar click Edit > Find TODO . All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the Search Results tab.

For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State *Searching (Aircraft)* before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability *Search* (source of the relationship).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked &quot;added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.</p><p>To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main toolbar click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find TODO</strong>.<br />All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4a2d810-8712-4983-8a2e-5d42a26d7ad6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State <em>Searching (Aircraft)</em> before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability <em>Search</em> (source of the relationship).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_of_capability_search.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using To Do property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000682 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02050: (2026x Refresh1) Using UAF and UPDM2 plugins in Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `304000682`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000682/2026x+Refresh1+Using+UAF+and+UPDM2+plugins+in+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:32.784+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Choosing the default plugin

Before the first startup of the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, you can set which plugin - UAF or UPDM2 - will be installed by default. If no changes are made, by default UAF plugin is installed.

To change the default plugin before the first startup

1. Go to the <cea_install_dir>\bin\ and open for editing the cameoea.properties file.
2. In the file, find the system property -Dstart.application.with.updm2.or.uaf.plugin=uaf
3. Change the uaf value to updm2 .
4. Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.

In case you will decide to change the plugin later, see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing between UAF and UPDM 2 plugins' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] for information about how to do that.

##### Displaying which plugin is installed

You can choose to display the plugin name in brackets next to the Cameo Enterprise Architecture title in the modeling tool's title bar.

To display the installed plugin name near the modeling tool title

1. Go to the <cea_install_dir>\bin\ and open for editing the cameoea.properties file.
2. In the file, find the system property -Dadd.plugin.name.to.application.title\=false
3. Change the property value to true .
4. Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Choosing the default plugin</h3><p>Before the first startup of the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, you can set which plugin - UAF or UPDM2 - will be installed by default. If no changes are made, by default UAF plugin is installed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the default plugin before the first startup</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the &lt;cea_install_dir&gt;\bin\ and open for editing the <em>cameoea.properties</em> file.</li><li>In the file, find the system property <em>-Dstart.application.with.updm2.or.uaf.plugin=uaf</em></li><li>Change the <em>uaf</em> value to <em>updm2</em>.</li><li>Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c93f7330-b046-4e85-be90-7075c3f9d206"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In case you will decide to change the plugin later, see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" ri:content-title="Changing between UAF and UPDM 2 plugins" /></ac:link> for information about how to do that.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Displaying which plugin is installed</h3><p>You can choose to display the plugin name in brackets next to the Cameo Enterprise Architecture title in the modeling tool's title bar. </p><p><br /></p><p>To display the installed plugin name near the modeling tool title</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the &lt;cea_install_dir&gt;\bin\ and open for editing the <em>cameoea.properties</em> file.</li><li>In the file, find the system property <em>-Dadd.plugin.name.to.application.title\=false</em></li><li>Change the <em>property </em>value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="plugin_name_in_title.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using UAF and UPDM2 plugins in Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991419 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02051: (2026x Refresh1) Using Units

- page_id: `303991419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991419/2026x+Refresh1+Using+Units
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:42.769+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Unit represents a standard unit of measure. For example,metre, kilometre, or foot are units of length.Units are used to specify [Value Properties](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919459/Value+Property).This section explains how to specify units for a Value Property, how to display or hide units, and unit symbols in various places.

##### Specifying Unit for a Value Property

When a Block has a Value Property defined, it requires a numeric value and must be typed by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Type' space='MED'], either with or without (unitless) a specified Unit.

To specify the Unit for a Value Property

1. Select the Value Property in the Block compartments.
2. If the ISO-80000 library is not uploaded in your project, click the**ISO** button on the smart manipulator toolbar. If you work with projects older than version 18.0, which use the QUDV library and SysML 1.3 library, the ISO-80000 library will not be compatible.If you work with Teamwork projects, the ISO-80000 library is uploaded by default. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Open the **Select Type** menu inone of the following way:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Press Ctrl+T.
  - From the shortcut menu, select **Type**.
4. Select the Value Type by typing the quantity name and unit name in the following syntax: quantity name[unit name]. Unit libraries- If you cannot find a suitable Unit in the standard libraries, you can create custom Units. To learn more about creating units, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Units' space=''] page. 
 - You can manage the scope of provided units with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MED'] feature. The unit is specified for a Value Property. As an example, see the figure below. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *stoppingDistance* Value Property typed by *distance[metre]* Value Type.

##### Displaying/hiding Units

After the unit is specified you can:

- Display/hide units for Value Property.
- 
- Display/hide unit symbolsfor values.

###### value_propertyDisplaying/hiding units for Value Property

The full name of the unit (e.g. metre, kilogram) is shown by default next to the Value Property on the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''].

To hide/display the full name of a unit for a Value Property

1. Select the Block shape.
2. From its shortcut menu, select **Symbol Properties**.
3. Select the **All** options display mode.
4. Find the**Show Attributes Tagged Values** option.
5. Set its value to *true* to show the unit (default), or *false*to hide it. The full name of the unit is shown/hidden next to the values in brackets in the compartments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### symbolDisplaying/hiding unit symbols for Value Property

You can show the unit symbol in brackets (e.g. (m), (kg)) next to the Value Property on the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space='']. If the Value Property has a value defined, the unit symbol in brackets is hidden (see the figure below). >]]>

To display/hide a unit symbol next to a Value Property

1. Select the Block shape.
2. From its shortcut menu, select **Symbol Properties**.
3. Find the**Show Unit on Value Property** option.
4. Set its value to *true* to show the unit symbol, or *false*(default) to hide it. The unit symbol is shown/hidden next to the Value Property. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### valuesDisplaying/hiding units symbols for values

You can hide or display a unit's symbol (e.g. m, kg):

- 
- 
- 

**on blockDisplaying/hiding unit symbols next to values**

You can display or hide the unit symbols (e.g. m, kg) of values in the entire project when the Unit is already defined for the Value Property and it has a value specified. For projects older than version 18.5, you must enable the **Show Units** option in the **Project Options** dialog if you want to see unit symbols. As of version 18.5, this option is enabled by default for any new projects.

show_unitsTo display/hide unit symbols next to values

1. Select **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, expand the **General** option group and select **SysML**.
3. Set the **Show Units** option value to *true* to show unit symbols, or *false*to hide. 
The unit symbols are shown next to the values in the compartments. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

**slotDisplaying/hiding unit symbols next to slots**

You can display a unit symbol for a slot on the Instance Specification shape by specifying it in the following ways:

- Using the **Specify Type** button from the smart manipulator toolbar.
- .
- .

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

The following procedures work only if the **Show Units** option is enabled. How to enable the **Show Units** option >

typeTo specify or change the unit symbol of a slot using the **Specify Type** button

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select the value in the compartment, and click the **Specify****Type** button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Right-click the value in the compartment, and select **Type**.
  - Select the value in the compartment, and press Ctrl+T.
2. Specify the new unit, or select an existing unit from the **Select Type** menu list. InformationThe list of units in the **Select Type** menu consists of those units that are defined in the Block property type, or in its subtypes. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

manuallyTo specify or change unit by typing it manually

1. Select the slot on the Instance Specification shape.
2. Click it to edit.
3. Type the required unit symbol next to the slot value.
4. Press Enter.

right-clickTo specify or change the unit using the right-click menu

1. Select the slot in the Instance Specification compartment, the row in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instance table' space=''], or the Slot in the Containment tree.
2. Right-click it, select the **Unit** button, and choose the required unit from the list. InformationThe list of units under the **Unit** button consists of those units that are defined either in the Block property type or in its subtypes.

**cells_columnsDisplaying unit symbols in the Instance table**

You can display units in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instance table' space=''] in the following ways:

- 
- 

unit_in_cellTo show unit symbols in the cells of an Instance Table

1. Click the **Options** button.
2. Select the **Show Units on Values** option. 
The unit symbols are displayed next to the values in the cells.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Show Units on Values

Show Unit

Project Options

Show Units

unit_in_columnTo show the unit symbol in the column header of the Instance Table

1. Click the **Options** button.
2. Select the **Show Units on Columns**option. 
The unit symbols are displayed on the column header.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span><span class="conf-macro output-inline" style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" data-macro-name="excerpt-include">A Unit represents a standard <span>unit of measure. For example,<span> metre, kilometre, or foot are units of <span>length.<span> Units are used to specify <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919459/Value+Property">Value Properties</a>.</span></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> This section explains how to specify units for a Value Property, how to display or hide units, and unit symbols in various places.</span></span></p><h3><span>Specifying Unit for a Value Property<br /></span></h3><p><span> When a Block has a Value Property defined, it requires a numeric value and must be typed by the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Value Type" /></ac:link>, either with or without (unitless) a specified Unit. <br /></span></p><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To specify the Unit for a Value Property</span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the Value Property in the Block compartments.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If the ISO-80000 library is not uploaded in your project, click the<strong> ISO</strong> button on the smart manipulator toolbar.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3c05294-e3a9-46ac-9f04-52320826559c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>If you work with projects older than version 18.0, which use the QUDV library and SysML 1.3 library, the ISO-80000 library will not be compatible.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you work with Teamwork projects, the ISO-80000 library is uploaded by default.</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="143"><ri:attachment ri:filename="iso_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Units" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open the <strong>Select Type</strong> menu in </span></span>one of the following way:<br /></span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image ac:title="Specify Type" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Specify Type" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_type.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Units" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Press Ctrl+T.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Type</strong>.<br /></span></li></ul></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the Value Type by t</span></span>yping the quantity name and unit name in the following syntax: quantity name[unit name].</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70fa28d6-f24e-4612-acd8-2581559432b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Unit libraries</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> - If you cannot find a suitable Unit in the standard libraries, you can create custom Units. To learn more about creating units, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Units" /></ac:link> page.<br /> - You can manage the scope of provided units with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Package import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> feature.<br /></span></span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The unit is specified for a Value Property. As an example, see the figure below.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_unit_for_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Units" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The <em>stoppingDistance</em> Value Property typed by <em>distance[metre]</em> Value Type.</span></h6><h3>Displaying/hiding Units</h3><p>After the unit is specified you can:</p><ul><li><span class="toc-item-body"><ac:link ac:anchor="value_property"><ac:link-body><span class="toc-link">Display/hide units for</span> Value Property.</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><ac:link ac:anchor="symbol"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display/hide unit symbols for Value Property.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="values"><ac:link-body><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-link">Display/hide unit symbols </span>for values</span>.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f20e55b-f20c-4708-abc7-242f81495829"><ac:parameter ac:name="">value_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying/hiding units for Value Property</h4><p>The full name of the unit (e.g. metre, kilogram) is shown by default next to the Value Property on the Block <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To hide/display the full name of a unit</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">for a Value Property</span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the Block shape.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From its shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the <strong>All</strong> options display mode.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Find the<strong> Show Attributes Tagged Values</strong> option.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Set its value to <em>true</em> to show the unit (default), or <em>false </em>to hide it.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The full name of the unit is shown/hidden next to the values in brackets in the compartments.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_attributes_tagged_values_option.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Units" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44d8a4ce-feea-4b07-a4b5-950b0ee8d0f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">symbol</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying/hiding unit symbols for Value Property</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can show the unit symbol in brackets (e.g. (m), (kg)) next to the Value Property on the Block <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. If the Value Property has a value defined, the unit symbol in brackets is hidden (see the figure below). <ac:link ac:anchor="on block"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to show unit symbols next to values >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To display/hide a unit</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">symbol next to a Value Property</span></span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the Block shape.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From its shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Find the<strong> Show Unit on Value Property</strong> option.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Set its value to <em>true</em> to show the unit symbol, or <em>false </em>(default) to hide it.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The unit symbol is shown/hidden next to the Value Property.</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="unit_symbol_in_brackets.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Units" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b9ec9ed-a16d-4567-8880-c314ea9fcdde"><ac:parameter ac:name="">values</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying/hiding units symbols for values</h4><p>You can hide or display a unit's symbol (e.g. m, kg):</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="on block"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Next to values in the whole project.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="slot"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Next to slots on the Instance Specification shape.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="cells_columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In cells and column headers of the Instance Table.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><br /></span></li></ul><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ffa5635-8dcd-4464-bd41-934624c5e374"><ac:parameter ac:name="">on block</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying/hiding unit symbols next to values<br /></strong></p><p>You can display or hide the unit symbols (e.g. m, kg) of values in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the entire project</span> when the Unit is already defined for the Value Property and it has a value specified. For projects older than version 18.5, you must enable the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Show Units</strong> option in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog if you want to see unit symbols. As of version 18.5, this option is enabled by default for any new projects.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" <strong>Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> option group and select <strong>SysML</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Set the <strong>Show Units</strong> option value to <em>true</em> to show unit symbols, or <em>false </em>to ## PAGE 02052: (2026x Refresh1) Validating project integrity
````

- page_id: `303992113`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992113/2026x+Refresh1+Validating+project+integrity

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

762190336

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

762190338

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

762190337

Some aspects are validated automatically, such as:

- Mount location inconsistencies in used projects
- Required version inconsistencies in used projects

##### Mount location inconsistencies in used projects

This occurs when packages of the used project have different mount locations defined in separate projects.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The used project *Tools* has mounted a package of the used project *ComponentLibrary*, while the used project *MainBlocks* has also mounted the same package *ComponentsLibrary*. As shown in the following figure, the mount location conflict occurs because the project*RadioReceiverProject*is using both the *Tools*and *MainBlocks*used projects at the same time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can solve the conflict in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='CM']**U****sed Project**dialogby unifying the**Mounted On**value in both projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of conflicting mount locations warning displayed in Used Project dialog.

##### Required version inconsistencies in used projects

This occurs when a project directly and indirectly uses several different versions of the same used project at the same time.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The used project *Tools* is using the 4th version of the used project *ComponentLibrary* and the used project *MainBlocks* is using the 2nd version of the used project *ComponentLibrary*. In the project *RadioReceiverProject*, a used project *ComponentLibrary* version conflict occurs as two different versions of this project are required at the same time (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can solve the conflict in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects' space='']**U****sed Project**dialogby unifying the required versions in both projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of conflicting versions warning displayed in Used Project dialog

##### Cyclic dependencies among used projects

The figure below illustrates an example of cyclic dependency:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of cyclic dependency.

The Project Integrity Correctness suite includes the rules for **detecting** Cyclic dependencies among used projects.While [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation' space='']the model, use the **Project Integrity Correctness** validation suite to find cyclic dependency issues in your project composition.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project Integrity Correctness suite

Violations of your project integrity will be displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of violations detected while checking project integrity correctness

You can resolve the cyclic dependency issues in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects' space='']**Used Project** dialog or you can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='MCS'].

762197964

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects' space='']

## PAGE 02053: (2026x Refresh1) Validation

- page_id: `303990305`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990305/2026x+Refresh1+Validation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:35.127+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis
- labels: ['validation', 'active-validation', 'validation-rules', 'validation-suites', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Purpose

Validation checks the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model, marks invalid elements in the model, and suggests solutions.

##### Validation suites

Models are validated against a set of validation rules. Each validation rule captures an imperative condition that must be checked against the model. Validation rules are grouped into meaningful groups called validation suites. There are predefined validation suites that you can customize. You can also create your own rules and group them into suites. Some validation rules are active; that is, they run all the time. Validation rules and suites can be exported into a separate project in order to reuse them. They are stored in the Teamwork Cloud server for exchange.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites' space='']>]]>
- Learn more about SysML validation suites >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Active validation suites' space='']>]]>
- Learn more about SysML active validation suites >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Requirements validation suites' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation suites' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating new validation rules' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Global validation rules' space='']>]]>

##### Validating your model

The validation can run on the entire model or on a selected part of it. All the elements in that scope are checked against relevant validation rules, and elements that violate these rules are reported in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel. They are also marked in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''], and their symbols are highlighted. Diagrams, tables, matrices, and relation maps displaying these highlighted symbols have the validation results marker bar.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Starting the validation' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation results marker bar' space='']Learn more about validation results marker bar >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']Learn more about Validation Results panel >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Pre-commit validation' space='']>]]>

##### Invalid elements representation

The invalid elements are marked as follows:

- [ATTACHMENT filename='error_icon.png'] - error or fatal error.
- [ATTACHMENT filename='warning_icon.png'] - warning.
- [ATTACHMENT filename='info_icon.png'] - debug or info.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing severity levels' space='']>]]>

You can see invalid elements in the following areas:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] an invalid element is marked with a small symbol that depends on the failure severity. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_element_in_model_brower.png']
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] an invalid symbol is highlighted. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_symbol_on_diagram_pane.png']
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation results marker bar' space=''] every marker of an invalid symbol is colored according to the violation severity. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_symbol_in_validation_results_marker_bar.png']
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] the failure indicator displays the severity icon, number of errors, and the first letter of the error severity (F - fatal error; E - error; W - warning; D - debug; I - info). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Solvers of invalid elements

Solvers of invalid elements

You can analyze invalid elements and solve problems by selecting appropriate commands from the invalid element or symbol shortcut menu or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel.

## PAGE 02054: (2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog

- page_id: `303996055`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996055/2026x+Refresh1+Validation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:22.298+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

60200619

60200621

60200618

**On this page**

43

60200620

INLINE

##### Introduction

The **Validation** dialog allows you to limit the scope of the constraints to validate against your model. Since the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Active validation suites' space=''] check your model all the time, the validation according [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites' space='']must be started by using this dialog. If you select the **All Validation Suite** as the **Validation Suite** in the **Validation** dialog, your model is validated against all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Active validation suites' space=''] at the same time. To limit the scope of the constraints to be validated against, select a more specific validation suite, such as *Numbering Validation*. The model or its part will be validated against the selected validation suite only. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel shows the results, listing all elements that do not conform to some constraints (invalid elements) in the selected validation suite.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Validation dialog. Its options (1,2,3,4,5) are described below.

##### Opening the Validation dialog

To open the **Validation** dialog

1. Do one of the following: - On the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Validate . - On the Validation toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
2. In the Validation dialog, specify the options. >]]>
3. Click Validate . The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite. The results are shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel .

You can run the validation for the entire diagram, table, matrix, relation map, or elements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Starting the validation' space='']>]]>

##### descrDescription of Validation dialog options

The **Validation** dialog consists of the following:

- The **Validation Suite** drop-down list (#1 in the image above) allows you to select a validation suite that you want to validate the model or its part against. The list of available validation suites depends on the open project; the validation suites and validation rules are stored in the model as regular model elements.
- The Validate For drop-down list (#2 in the image above) allows you to choose:
  - **Whole Project** to run validation on the entire model.
  - **Validation Selection** to run validation on special packages and/or elements. Then click the ... button and select the scope for validation. Validation is always recursive. If you select a package for validation, you do not need to select its inner elements.
- The Minimal Severity drop-down list (#3 in the image above) allows you to select the minimal severity level: debug , info , warning , error , fatal . Debug is the lowest possible severity level.
- Clear the Exclude elements from projects used in read-only mode check box (#4 in the image above) if you want to run validation on the elements stored in the read-only project usages.
- Clear the Exclude elements from additional content check box (#5 in the image above) if you want to include the content of Smart Packages while running the passive validation.
- The Validation Suites button (#6 in the image above) opens the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Suites dialog' space='']**Validation Suites** dialog .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8cabc80-59ec-4ce2-8756-eb0c214e0b55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">60200619</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da12eb45-022c-4751-b345-b7bf3dd35b61"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">60200621</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="977fe717-a51b-411b-a3fe-55cc08a00c4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">60200618</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d59b801-6e47-46c0-a68b-a4654d35fe48"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dd257d0-51cd-405e-850a-024ccfee01cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">60200620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="753012cd-3b46-42d6-9bcc-418ead40be87"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Introduction</h3><p>The <strong>Validation</strong> dialog allows you to limit the scope of the constraints to validate against your model. Since the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Active validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active validation suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> check your model all the time, the validation according <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined validation suites ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>must be started by using this dialog. If you select the <strong>All Validation Suite</strong> as the <strong>Validation Suite</strong> in the <strong>Validation</strong> dialog, your model is validated against all <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Active validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active validation suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> at the same time. To limit the scope of the constraints to be validated against, select a more specific validation suite, such as <em>Numbering Validation</em>. The model or its part will be validated against the selected validation suite only. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link> shows the results, listing all elements that do not conform to some constraints (invalid elements) in the selected validation suite.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Validation dialog. Its options (1,2,3,4,5) are described below.</h6><h3>Opening the Validation dialog</h3><p>To open the <strong>Validation</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br />  - On the main menu, select <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>Validate</strong>.<br />  - On the Validation toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation</strong> dialog, specify the options. <ac:link ac:anchor="descr"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Descriptions of options >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Click <strong>Validate</strong>.<br />The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite. The results are shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a46a9f96-3f4a-44c5-acb4-0d47ae6fcf39"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can run the validation for the entire diagram, table, matrix, relation map, or elements. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Starting the validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to start the validation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="429df29b-df6b-40de-8465-6d2fcd1594c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">descr</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Description of Validation dialog options</h3><p>The <strong>Validation</strong> dialog consists of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Validation Suite</strong> drop-down list (#1 in the image above) allows you to select a validation suite that you want to validate the model or its part against.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="caf7c18f-d146-4ec2-9d29-6c0dc96d1a0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The list of available validation suites depends on the open project; the validation suites and validation rules are stored in the model as regular model elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>The <strong>Validate For</strong> drop-down list (#2 in the image above) allows you to choose:<ul><li><p><strong>Whole Project</strong> to run validation on the entire model.</p></li><li><p><strong>Validation Selection</strong> to run validation on special packages and/or elements. Then click the ... button and select the scope for validation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1aec3563-a243-4b5c-8ae2-f10c0cc09626"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Validation is always recursive. If you select a package for validation, you do not need to select its inner elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>The <strong>Minimal Severity </strong>drop-down list (#3 in the image above) allows you to select the minimal severity level: <strong>debug</strong>, <strong>info</strong>, <strong>warning</strong>, <strong>error</strong>, <strong>fatal</strong>. Debug is the lowest possible severity level.</li><li>Clear the<strong> Exclude elements from projects used in read-only mode</strong> check box (#4 in the image above) if you want to run validation on the elements stored in the read-only project usages.</li><li>Clear the <strong>Exclude elements from additional content </strong>check box (#5 in the image above) if you want to include the content of Smart Packages while running the passive validation.</li><li>The <strong>Validation Suites</strong> button (#6 in the image above) opens the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Suites dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 02056: (2026x Refresh1) Validation results marker bar

- page_id: `303996514`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996514/2026x+Refresh1+Validation+results+marker+bar
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:03.833+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation
- labels: ['validation-results-marker-bar', 'validation', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The validation results marker bar appears at the right side of a diagram pane, if the diagram contains symbols of invalid elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The markers are shown even for the items that are not currently visible on the screen (you may need to scroll down to see them). The marker bar shrinks to fit the screen and displays relative positions of annotated symbols on the axis of ordinates. Every marker is colored according to the violation severity.

Clicking a marker on the bar selects the appropriate symbol on the diagram pane. Moving the mouse over the marker eye (the red square at the top-right corner of the diagram pane) opens the summary of the diagram validation results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The marker bar shrinks to fit the screen and displays relative positions of annotated symbols on the axis of ordinates. Every marker is colored according to the violation severity.</p><p>Clicking a marker on the bar selects the appropriate symbol on the diagram pane. Moving the mouse over the marker eye (the red <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="97a32128-7416-4d36-b8bc-082522573e77">square</ac:inline-comment-marker> at the top-right corner of the diagram pane) opens the summary of the diagram validation results.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>

## PAGE 02057: (2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel

- page_id: `303996488`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996488/2026x+Refresh1+Validation+Results+panel
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:41.034+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1278320370

1278320374

**On this page**

43

1278320372

INLINE

INLINE

##### Introduction

The**Validation Results** panel and **Active Validation Results** panel display the results of the validation: invalid element, severity, explanation of the validation rule and its abbreviation. The **Active Validation Results** panel shows the results of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Active validation suites' space=''], while the **Validation Results** panel shows [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites' space=''].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Validation Results and Active Validation Results panels.

##### Opening the Validation Results panel

The **Validation Results** panel opens automatically after the validation has ended. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Starting the validation' space='']>]]>

Since the active validation suites always check your model's correctness, you must open the **Active Validation Results** panel to see the results of active validation.

To open the **Active Validation Results** panel

- On the [status line](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Understanding+the+user+interface), click the failure indicator (e.g. [IMAGE alt='' src='']). If you cannot see the failure indicator on the status line, your model is correct according to active validation suites.

##### Description of Validation Results panel areas

The **Validation Results** panel consists of the following areas:

44

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Validation Results panel areas: toolbar, validation results, and summary of severity areas.

###### toolbarToolbar

The toolbar allows you to manage the validation results. All buttons are described in the following table.

| Button icon | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Expand All | Click to expand recursively all the elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Collapse All | Click to collapse recursively all the elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Navigate to Validated Object (Alt+B) | Click the button to select the element in the .Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following navigation commands:Open Specification - opens of the selected element in the validation results area.Go To - opens a submenu whose commands allow you to track and navigate to the elements. Select Rule in The Containment Tree - selects the failed validation rule in the of the selected element in the validation results area.Add to Search Results - selects an element in the . |
|  | Solve | Click to open the list of available commands to solve a selected element violation in the validation results area. |
|  | Ignore | Click to ignore a selected element violation in the validation results area. In server projects, you can select whether to ignore the violation for you as a project user or for all users who works on that server project.The button is available when you select an unignored element in the validation results area.Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following commands:Ignore Validation Rule - ignores the validation rule of a selected element in the validation results area.Ignore Validation Suite - ignores the validation suite of a selected element in the validation results area. |
|  | Unignore | Click to make a selected element in the validation results area violation unignored.The button is available when you select an ignored element in the validation results area. |
|  | Display the full path of the elements | Click to show the full path of all elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Run validation with current settings | Click to run the validation with current settings. |
|  | Run validation with new settings | Click to open the Validation dialog and run the validation with new settings. |
|  | Export | Click to export the list of validation results either to .html or .csv file. |

###### Validation results area

The validation results area provides a list of violated elements. This area contains the following columns:

- Element - elements that violate the validation rule.
- Severity - severity level of the validation rule.
- Abbreviation - a short word, phase, or acronym of the validation rule used for sorting/filtering.
- Message - description of the invalid situation when a validation rule fails.
- Is Ignored - indicator of whether the violation is ignored or not.

You can use the validation results area to:

- 
- 
- 

manageTo manage validation results

- Right-click the element in the validation results area and select one of the following commands from the shortcut menu:
  - Solver name
  - Ignore
  - Navigate to Validated Object
  - Open Specification
  - Go To
  - Select Rule in The Containment Tree

All those commands are described in the .

sortTo sort the validation results

- Click the header of the column you want to sort the validation results. A small arrow appears on the column header:
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='ascending_sort.png'] - ascending sorting.
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='decending_sort.png'] - descending sorting.

filterTo filter the validation results

1. Point to the header of a column whose value you want to select for filtering the validation results.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_validation_results.png'] at the right of the column header and select a value from the drop-down list.

###### Summary of severity area

The summary of the severity area displays the number and severity of violated elements in expanded packages. If you collapse the package in the validation results area, the number of violated elements are excluded from the count.

Results panel areas: toolbar, validation results, and summary of severity areas.</h6><h4><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ccfcf66-04ab-413a-82e0-73baa58473fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">toolbar</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Toolbar</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The toolbar allows you to manage the validation results. All buttons are described in the following table.</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 111.0px;" /><col style="width: 237.0px;" /><col style="width: 584.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button icon</th><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Expand All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Expand All" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Expand All</td><td>Click to expand recursively all the elements in the validation results area.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Collapse All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Collapse All" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collapse_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Collapse All</td><td>Click to collapse recursively all the elements in the validation results area.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigate_to_validated_object.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Navigate to Validated Object (Alt+B)</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button to select the element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following navigation commands:</p><ul><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>Open Specification</strong> - selects an element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Search Results tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Search Results tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<strong> </strong></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Solve" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Solve" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="solve.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Solve</p></div></td><td><p>Click to open the list of available commands to solve a selected element violation in the validation results area.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Ignore" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Ignore" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ignore.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Ignore</td><td><p>Click to ignore a selected element violation in the validation results area. In server projects, you can select whether to ignore the violation for you as a project user or for all users who works on that server project.</p><p>The button is available when you select an unignored element in the validation results area.</p><p>Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following commands:</p><ul><li><strong>Ignore Validation Rule</strong> - ignores the validation rule of a selected element in the validation results area.</li><li><strong>Ignore Validation Suite</strong> - ignores the validation suite of a selected element in the validation results area.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Unignore" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Unignore" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="unignore.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Unignore</td><td><p>Click to make a selected element in the validation results area violation unignored.</p><p>The button is available when you select an ignored element in the validation results area.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Display the full path of the elements" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Display the full path of the elements</p></td><td>Click to show the full path of all elements in the validation results area.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Run validation with current settings" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Run validation with current settings" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_validation_with_current_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Run validation with current settings</p></td><td>Click to run the validation with current settings.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Run validation with new settings" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Run validation with new settings" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_validation_with_new_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Run validation with new settings</p></td><td><p>Click to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Validation</strong></span> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and run the validation with new settings.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Export" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Export" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="export.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Export</td><td><p>Click to export the list of validation results either to .html or .csv file.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><h4>Validation results area</h4><p>The validation results area provides a list of violated elements. This area contains the following columns:</p><ul><li><strong>Element</strong> - elements that violate the validation rule.</li><li><strong>Severity</strong> - severity level of the validation rule.</li><li><strong>Abbreviation</strong> - a short word, phase, or acronym of the validation rule used for sorting/filtering.</li><li><strong>Message</strong> - description of the invalid situation when a validation rule fails.</li><li><strong>Is Ignored</strong> - indicator of whether the violation is ignored or not.</li></ul><p>You can use the validation results area to:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="manage"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage validation results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="sort"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sort validation results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="filter"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filter validation results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9336097-713a-4edb-bc04-a87463283b8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">manage</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To manage validation results</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the element in the validation results area and select one of the following commands from the shortcut menu:<ul><li>Solver name</li><li><strong>Ignore</strong></li><li><strong>Navigate to Validated Object</strong></li><li><strong>Open Specification</strong></li><li><strong>Go To</strong></li><li><strong>Select Rule in The Containment Tree <br /><br /></strong></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c19c4f64-3fe0-489e-99f5-6813e0498cba"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All those commands are described in the <ac:link ac:anchor="toolbar"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table above]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e08f7231-142d-4271-8d7b-254ef4532431"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sort</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To sort the validation results</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the header of the column you want to sort the validation results. A small arrow appears on the column header:<ul><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="28"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ascending_sort.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>- ascending sorting.</li><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="28"><ri:attachment ri:filename="decending_sort.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>- descending sorting.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83be7782-8da8-408a-82d0-698d6ff1aef8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">filter</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To filter the validation results</p><hr /><ol><li>Point to the header of a column whose value you want to select for filtering the validation results.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_validation_results.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the right of the column header and select a value from the drop-down list.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Summary of severity area</h4><p>The summary of the severity area displays the number and severity of violated elements in expanded packages. If you collapse the package in the validation results area, the number of violated elements are excluded from the count.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999178 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02058: (2026x Refresh1) Validation Suites dialog

- page_id: `303999178`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999178/2026x+Refresh1+Validation+Suites+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:38.595+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation > (2026x Refresh1) Custom validation > (2026x Refresh1) Creating validation suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Validation Suites** dialog allows you to create new suites, clone read-only validation suites, include one suite to another, and include/exclude already existing validation rules in validation suites.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Validation Suites dialog areas: toolbar, Validation Suites, and Validation Rules areas.

##### Opening the Validation Suites dialog

To open the **Validation Suites** dialog

- Do either: - From the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Validation Suites . - Open the **Validation** dialog and click the Validation Suites button.

##### Description of Validation Suites dialog areas

The **Validation Suites** dialog consists of the areas described below.

###### toolToolbar

The toolbar allows you to manage the validation suites. All buttons are described in the following table.

| Button icon | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Create New Validation Suite | Click to create a new validation suite. This button contains the following commands:Validation Suite - creates a new validation suite, stored in your model as a Package with «validationSuite» stereotype applied. The validation rules stored in this suite will check if a model is correct and complete only when you start your model validation manually. >]]>Active Validation Suite - creates a new active validation suite stored in your model as a Package with «activeValidationSuite» stereotype applied. The validation rules stored in this suite will be active and check if a model is correct and complete immediately. >]]> |
|  | Clone Validation Suite | Click to clone an already existing validation suite with its validation rules. It is stored in your model as a Package with «validationSuite» stereotype applied. |
|  | Rename (F2) | Click to rename the package of new or cloned validation suites. |
|  | Remove | Click to delete the new or cloned validation suites from the model. |

###### Validation Suites area

The Validation Suites area shows a list of all validation suites that comes with a profile (read-only) or your own created validation suites (editable).

Each profile contains its own validation suites.

You can manage validation suites directly in the Validation Suites area by using the or shortcut menu commands: **Create Validation Suite**, **Create Active Validation Suite**,**Clone Validation Suite**, **Rename (F2)**, or **Remove**.

All those commands are described in the .

###### Validation Rules area

The Validation Rules area contains:

- *Constraints Tree*displaying the structure of the validation suite Packages with their validation rules. It allows you to include/exclude validation rules to/from a newly created or cloned validation suite Package. For this select/clear the check box before the entire Package or an appropriate validation rule. You cannot include/exclude the validation rules to/from read-only validation suites.
- Buttons :

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Edit | Click to open the of the selected element in the Constraint Tree. |
| Load | Click to load the with its validation rules. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991513 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02059: (2026x Refresh1) Value Propagation

- page_id: `303991513`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991513/2026x+Refresh1+Value+Propagation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:46.008+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Initial Values
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The value propagation mechanism enables values that are not overridden by the values from the selected context in the *initialValue* compartment to be displayed. Assuming the property and the Value Propagation options are enabled, the value available in the next context will be used to reconfigure the property if there is no value in the selected context to reconfigure the property. However, if there is no value available in any context, the class-level default value will be displayed in the *initialValue* compartment, indicating that the property is not reconfigured at all.

The value propagation mechanism is enabled by default. To disable this mechanism, follow the procedure below.

To disable the value propagation mechanism

1. On the main toolbar, click Options > Project . .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > SysML .
3. Select the Propagate SysML Values property value to false .
4. Click OK .

#### TIP: Example when the value propagation mechanism is enabled

Example when the value propagation mechanism is enabled

In the UniverseContext package, only the value of x of a Location is reconfigured to 3 in the UniverseContext context. The values of y and z are not set by the selected context. Since the value propagation is enabled, the next context, Universe, is considered. In the Universe context, the value of z is set to 2. However, the value of y is still missing; therefore, the next context, Thing, is considered. In the Thing context, the value of y is set to 1. Now, all attributes of the Location are set as follows:

- x = 3
- y = 1
- z = 2

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The value propagation mechanism enables values that are not overridden by the values from the selected context in the <em>initialValue</em> compartment to be displayed. Assuming the property and the Value Propagation options are enabled, the value available in the next context will be used to reconfigure the property if there is no value in the selected context to reconfigure the property. However, if there is no value available in any context, the class-level default value will be displayed in the <em>initialValue</em> compartment, indicating that the property is not reconfigured at all.</p><p>The value propagation mechanism is enabled by default. To disable this mechanism, follow the procedure below.</p><p>To disable the value propagation mechanism</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main toolbar, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. .</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Propagate SysML Values</strong> property value to<em>false</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bc5f836-d58e-41be-8162-97be77a1bb60"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example when the value propagation mechanism is enabled</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the UniverseContext package, only the value of x of a Location is reconfigured to 3 in the UniverseContext context. The values of y and z are not set by the selected context. Since the value propagation is enabled, the next context, Universe, is considered. In the Universe context, the value of z is set to 2. However, the value of y is still missing; therefore, the next context, Thing, is considered. In the Thing context, the value of y is set to 1. Now, all attributes of the Location are set as follows:</p><ul><li>x = 3</li><li>y = 1</li><li>z = 2</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2017-4-25_11-26-56.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Value Propagation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
